zh.lifeSummary: 當John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar於1527年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特時，他的父親Harold Rossell27歲，母親Dorothy Cranmer34歲。他於大約1550年與Annora Pierrepont在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1598年在他的家鄉享年72歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar于1527年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特时，他的父亲Harold Rossell27岁，母亲Dorothy Cranmer34岁。他于大约1550年与Annora Pierrepont在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1598年在他的家乡享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rossell,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rosell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Teobaldo, Jorge, Pedro, Santiago, Ana, Carlos, Ernesto, Jose, Miguel, Modesto, Alberto.  Scandinavian Erik, Nils.
Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element ros ‘rose’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .

variants.name: Rozzell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Rossell   or   Russell  . This surname is now rare in Britain.
Americanized form of   Rossel   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Rozzelle  .

variants.name: Rosselle
meanings: Altered form of English   Rossell  .
French: from the feminine form of   Rossel   and a variant of   Rousselle  . Compare   Rozelle  .

variants.name: Rosel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Felicitas, Araceli, Armando, Arturo, Cristina, Esequiel, Francisco, Margarita, Nestor, Paciano, Roberto, Rodelio.
Catalan: variant of   Rossell  .
German (Rösel): from a pet form of Rose 1.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Rossel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Fernando, Juan, Alvaro, Ana, Carols, Catarino, Guillermo, Javier, Jose, Orlando, Pedro, Ruben.  French Philippe, Yves.  Dutch Gerrit, Flor.
French, Dutch, and Flemish: nickname for a red-haired man, from a diminutive of Old French and Dutch ros ‘red’.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Catalan   Rossell  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Ratcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Ratliff  .

variants.name: Routley
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Cornwall): apparently a habitational name from Great Rutleigh in Northlew (Devon), although this is recorded as Radeclive in medieval documents (see Ratcliffe ) and is not found as Rutleigh until as late as 1809. The surname evidence suggests that the placename may have already developed informally into Rutleigh during the 15th or 16th century. The placename derives from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: on
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Lam, Muoi, Hai, Hoa, Loc, Trung, Be Van, Binh, Ha, Huu, Phoung, Tong.  Chinese Meng, Dung, Hong, Kin, Kwong, Kyung-Won, Mi Ok, Wing, Yan, Yon, Young.
Vietnamese (Ôn): from the Chinese surname 溫, see Wen 1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 安, see   An   1.

variants.name: Wen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Han, Cheng, Jin, Li, Yen, Yong, Chen, Chih-Hung, Chin, Hong, Jian, Jung, Long, Huan, Hung, Hung Chi, Ly, Quan.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 溫, meaning ‘warm’ in Chinese: (i) from Wen (溫), the name of an ancient state during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). During the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) this state was annexed by the state of Di and the prince of Wen fled to the state of Wey (located around present-day Hebi and Xinxiang in Henan province). His descendants acquired 溫, the name of their original state, as their surname. (ii) from the placename Wen (溫), the name of a fief granted to Wen Ji (溫季), an official in the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the Chi Wen (叱溫), Wen Pen (溫盆), and Wen Gu (溫孤) families of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) from the personal name of Wen Sheng (溫昇), who went into exile and changed his original surname, Liu (劉), to Wen 溫 after his father, Liu Yicong (劉易從), an official in the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), suffered persecution and death.

nameMeanings.name: Soar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
nickname for someone with reddish brown hair, from Middle English sor(e), sour ‘reddish brown’ (Old French sor); compare Sorrell .
topographic name for someone who lived in or by a muddy place, from Middle English sour, sore ‘mud, slime’ (Old Norse saurr).

variants.name: Sorrell
meanings: English (Middlesex and Essex): nickname for someone with reddish-brown hair, from Middle English and Old French sorel ‘reddish-brown, chestnut-colored’ (see Sorel ).
Altered form of French Canadian   Sorel   2 and, in some cases, possibly also of French   Sorel   1, a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: When John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar was born in 1527, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Harold Rossell, was 27 and his mother, Dorothy Cranmer, was 34. He married Annora Pierrepont about 1550, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died about 1598, in his hometown, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar est né en 1527, à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Harold Rossell, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Cranmer, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Annora Pierrepont environ 1550, à Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé environ 1598, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar(이)가 1527년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Harold Rossell(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Cranmer(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 Annora Pierrepont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1598년 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Rossell, of Ratcliffe on Soar родился в 1527 г. в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Harold Rossell было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Cranmer, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Annora Pierrepont около 1550 г. в местности холм пьиррепонт, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер около 1598 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет.
